note: The narrator's triage queue (shell command `hint`). First entry whose `if` passes is the current hint — ordered earliest gate to latest, each conditioned on that step being incomplete. Nudge, don't solve: point at the room or the noun, let the player earn the click.

hints.text: Rule one of any incident: confirm it's not just you. Your equipment is right there, and you own a cable you trust more than most people.
A professional travels with kit. Yours is filed wherever you file things you'd want in a fire — which, knowing you, is a drawer.
The world outside has a dress code, technically. Somewhere in this house is a pair of Real Pants with your name on them. The bedroom knows more than it's saying.
Somebody in this town knows where the internet physically lives. In a town this size, information eats at one counter. Ask the man who predates the infrastructure — ask him about the internet.
The trail points east, past the dead traffic light. Walk it. The hum will do the rest.
The gray door wants a key, and installers always leave a spare — with paperwork to match. Young men in hurries leave their paperwork in the nearest large green container.

You know the rule: the combo is the last four of the NOC hotline. Corporate printed that number on every glossy thing they ever sent this town — and this town never throws away anything glossy. Check the notice board back on Main Street.
Rule plus number equals key. The box is where Kim said and where the clipping showed: on the gas meter, behind the plaza. The service drive wraps the right end of the building.
You are holding the janitor's key to the county's entire internet. The gray door has been waiting nine years. Introduce them.
Dark uplink, unknown cause — from the outside you can't tell a corpse from a mourner. Prove the local side first: your cable, the switch, any honest port.
The LAN answered. Now read the router's lights like a jury foreman and deliver the verdict.
The fault is upstream, and upstream has a street address. Something in this closet shipped here once — and paperwork never forgets where it came from.
Listen: under the hum, an engine, idling east of the plaza where the asphalt leaves town. Somebody in this county still drives out.

The act is done; the truck is aimed west. Anything left in the queue is curiosity — try `tickets` for the ledger, and LOOK at whatever you haven't. The narrator saves his best material for the thorough.
